text.title: Scope Drift Early-Warning Metrics — Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of Scope Drift Early-Warning Metrics must enforce.

rule.statement: The dashboard MUST compute all five named metrics nightly. Cherry-picking a subset is forbidden because each metric catches a different drift pattern.
rule.rationale: Each metric catches a different drift pattern; missing any one creates a blind spot the project will fall into.

rule.statement: Thresholds (green / amber / red) MUST be expressed relative to the signed baseline, not absolute counts.
rule.rationale: Absolute thresholds under-warn large projects and over-warn small ones; baseline-relative ratios work across sizes.
Weekly refresh is insufficient — drift accumulates within a single sprint.

rule.statement: Each amber and red threshold has a named trigger action with owner and SLA; red triggers route through `risk-threshold-policy`.
rule.rationale: A dashboard without actions is decoration; named actions turn each breach into a calendar event with an owner.

rule.statement: The dashboard has exactly one named owner who reviews it daily and acknowledges amber/red signals.
rule.rationale: Unowned dashboards produce unacted-upon red signals; ownership converts data into decisions.
